Name: gpu_inference_optimization
Title: Hardware-Faithful Optimization of Deep Learning Inference on Commodity GPUs
Short Hypothesis: By focusing on kernel-level throughput engineering and hardware-faithful evaluation, we can achieve significant speedups in deep learning inference on commodity GPUs through optimized memory-bandwidth utilization, KV-cache layout, and tensor-core occupancy.
Related Work: Previous work has focused on theoretical models and formal guarantees, but has often neglected the importance of hardware-faithful evaluation and empirical measurement. Our approach differs from existing work by prioritizing reproducible benchmarks, nvprof/Nsight traces, and end-to-end speedups on real hardware.
Abstract: This project proposes a novel approach to optimizing deep learning inference on commodity GPUs. By leveraging kernel-level throughput engineering and hardware-faithful evaluation, we aim to achieve significant speedups in inference time while maintaining accuracy. Our approach focuses on optimizing memory-bandwidth utilization, KV-cache layout, and tensor-core occupancy, and prioritizes reproducible benchmarks and empirical measurement. We will conduct experiments using nvprof/Nsight traces and report end-to-end speedups on real hardware, ensuring that our results are both meaningful and deployable.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Optimizing memory-bandwidth utilization through coalesced loads and reduced HBM round-trips
Experiment 2: Improving KV-cache layout through autotuned tile sizes and profiled per-layer precision
Experiment 3: Enhancing tensor-core occupancy through avoided bank conflicts and better register allocation
Risk Factors and Limitations: Limited access to specialized hardware or expertise
Potential for overfitting to specific hardware configurations
Difficulty in reproducing results across different driver/CUDA versions
